hero.subtitle: DevFest Santo Domingo 2026
hero.title:
Where developers
build the future.
hero.description: Two days of deep-dive talks on AI, Cybersecurity, Testing, Data engineering, and UI/UX — built by the Dominican dev community, for the Dominican dev community.

eventInfo.dateDesc: Two full days of tech talks, workshops, and networking with the local community.
eventInfo.venueDesc: Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic — well-equipped and accessible spaces.
eventInfo.formatDesc: Attend in person on campus and enjoy the full experience alongside the community, with networking opportunities, activities, and direct access to every talk and event activity.

about.title: Community-powered. Globally connected.
about.devfestText: DevFest is the annual tech conference organized by Google Developer Groups around the world. Each edition brings together developers, designers, and tech enthusiasts to explore the latest in Android, Web, Cloud, AI/ML, and community-driven technology.
about.gdgText: Google Developer Groups are community-run chapters for developers interested in Google technologies. GDG Santo Domingo is the local chapter for the Dominican Republic — we host meetups, workshops, study jams, and of course, DevFest.

cfp.title: Got something to share?
cfp.description: Submit your talk proposal and be part of the biggest GDG event in the Caribbean. We are looking for talks on AI, Cybersecurity, Testing, Data, UI/UX, and anything pushing tech forward.

talk.title: Expert Talks
talk.desc: Learn from industry leaders and innovators sharing their insights and experiences.

workshop.title: Hands-on Workshops
workshop.desc: Get practical experience with the latest technologies and frameworks.

networking.title: Networking
networking.desc: Connect with fellow developers, entrepreneurs, and tech enthusiasts.

awards.title: Awards & Recognition
awards.desc: Celebrate excellence in the developer community.

pricing.title: Get your ticket
pricing.subtitle: Pick your tier. All tickets include access to the main stage, lunch, and the afterparty.

schedulePage.heroTitle:
Two days of talks,
workshops & community
schedulePage.heroSubtitle: Explore 40+ sessions across AI, Cybersecurity, Testing, Data Engineering, and UI/UX. Filter by track and plan your two days at DevFest Santo Domingo.
schedulePage.title: Schedule
schedulePage.subtitle: Two days. Six tracks. 25+ talks.
schedulePage.lunchSubtitle: Sponsored by Google Cloud — food trucks on the patio
schedulePage.afterPartySubtitle: Drinks, hallway conversations, and live DJ — badge required
schedulePage.noSessions: No sessions found for this filter.
schedulePage.ctaDesc: Download the full two-day program as an .ics file and drop it straight into Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, or Outlook.

speakersPage.heroSubtitle: 40+ engineers, designers, and researchers from across Latin America and beyond — sharing what they're building, breaking, and learning right now.
speakersPage.keynoteDesc: Two days. Two opening keynotes from people who don't just write the specs — they ship the systems that millions of developers build on top of.
speakersPage.cfpBadge: CALL FOR PAPERS · OPEN UNTIL APR 30
speakersPage.ctaSubtext: We're still looking for talks, lightning sessions, and workshops on AI, cloud, security, data engineering, and more.

teamPage.headline:
Built by the
community, for
the community.
teamPage.subtitle: DevFest Santo Domingo runs on volunteer time. Sixty-plus organizers, captains, and helpers across eight GDG chapters — these are the people who make the weekend happen.
teamPage.title: Our Team
teamPage.coreTeamSubtitle: The four people steering the ship — programming, operations, content, and the GDG chapter behind it all.
teamPage.committeeSubtitle: Twelve people running the tracks, partner relationships, and the operational threads that hold a two-day conference together.
teamPage.volCrewSubtitle: 45+ volunteers across five workstreams — handling check-in, the mic line, livestream, signage, and everything in between.
teamPage.ctaHeadline: Want to be on this page next year?
teamPage.ctaSubtitle: Volunteer roles open in May. No prior experience required — just a willingness to help, learn, and meet a lot of people building things on the island.

conductPage.headline:
A safe space
for every developer.
conductPage.subtitle: DevFest Santo Domingo is a community event for everyone — regardless of background, identity, or experience level. By attending, speaking, sponsoring, or volunteering, you agree to follow the rules below.
conductPage.pledgeSubtitle: These aren't suggestions. They're the floor — the minimum we expect from everyone who shares space with our community for two days.
conductPage.inclusive_desc: Welcome people of every race, gender, orientation, ability, age, and experience level. Assume good faith.
conductPage.respectful_desc: Disagree on ideas, never on people. Listen actively. Use names and pronouns people share with you.
conductPage.safe_desc: Zero tolerance for harassment, intimidation, threats, or unsolicited photography. Consent is mandatory.
conductPage.helpful_desc: Lift others up. Mentor where you can. Celebrate first-time speakers, junior devs, and people new to our community.
conductPage.reportTitle:
If something goes wrong,
tell us.
conductPage.enforceTitle: What happens if someone violates the code.
conductPage.warning_desc: For minor or first-time issues. The reported person is contacted privately, told what happened, and asked to stop.
conductPage.publicWarning_desc: A formal warning, with the offending behavior named. Continued behavior moves directly to step 3 or 4.
conductPage.removal_desc: For serious incidents: the person is asked to leave the venue. No refund. Speakers' talks may be removed from the program.
conductPage.ban_desc: For severe or repeat violations: a permanent ban from all GDG Santo Domingo events and online community spaces.
conductPage.title: Code of Conduct
conductPage.reportSectionSubtitle: If something happens during the event — or has already happened — pick whichever channel feels safest. All reports are taken seriously and handled by a dedicated CoC response team within 24 hours.
conductPage.enforcementSubtitle: Every report is unique, but here's the general ladder we use. The CoC team has full discretion to skip steps depending on severity.
conductPage.attrText: This Code of Conduct adapts language from the Contributor Covenant v2.1 and the GDG global community guidelines, with localizations specific to Santo Domingo and the broader Dominican developer community.
conductPage.contactText: Reach the CoC committee — directly, privately, and on your terms.
